title: Minestrone Soup

subs.name: leek or the green tops of spring onion
subs.notes: celery has a distinct flavour but these substitutes work in the background of a hearty soup

subs.name: ridge gourd (turai), bottle gourd or green capsicum
subs.notes: ridge gourd is an excellent Indian stand-in for zucchini in soups

ingredients.name: cooked or canned kidney beans or cannellini beans

subs.name: boiled chickpeas, lobia or any available cooked legume
subs.notes: chickpeas add more texture; adjust simmer time if using dry soaked beans

subs.name: small pieces of broken wheat (daliya) or brown rice
subs.notes: daliya adds a pleasant nuttiness and is more nutritious

steps.text: Warm olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add the onion, celery and carrot, season with a pinch of salt and cook for 7–8 minutes until softened and lightly golden.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in the tomatoes, rosemary, thyme and bay leaf. Cook for 5 minutes until the tomatoes break down and the oil separates slightly at the edges.
Pour in the stock, bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a gentle simmer. Add the zucchini and beans. Partially cover and simmer for 15 minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
Add the pasta to the pot and cook uncovered for 8–10 minutes until the pasta is tender. The soup will thicken — add a little hot water if it becomes too thick for your liking.
Discard the bay leaf. Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Ladle into deep bowls and drizzle generously with a good olive oil before serving.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: rajma → boiled chickpeas, lobia or any available cooked legume (chickpeas add more texture; adjust simmer time if using dry soaked beans); zucchini → ridge gourd (turai), bottle gourd or green capsicum (ridge gourd is an excellent Indian stand-in for zucchini in soups); pasta → small pieces of broken wheat (daliya) or brown rice (daliya adds a pleasant nuttiness and is more nutritious); celery → leek or the green tops of spring onion (celery has a distinct flavour but these substitutes work in the background of a hearty soup)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Make this in a pressure cooker: sauté the soffritto (onion, celery, carrot, garlic) in the cooker base on medium flame, then add tomatoes, vegetables, stock and beans. Seal and pressure-cook for 3 whistles on medium. Open after pressure releases, return to the flame, add pasta or broken wheat and simmer open for 10 minutes. Knorr or Maggi vegetable cubes dissolved in water work as stock. Zucchini can be replaced with turai (ridge gourd) which behaves identically in soup.
